AESTDTC variable definition row absent from candidate set.

PDF TABLE_ROW 'AEENDTC | End Date/Time of Adverse Event | Char | ISO 8601 datetime or interval | Timing | End date/time of the adverse event represented in ISO 8601 character format. | Exp' not present in MD assumptions.md candidate atoms. AEENDTC variable row absent.

PDF TABLE_ROW 'AEENDY | Study Day of End of Adverse Event | Num |  | Timing | Study day of end of event relative to the sponsor-defined RFSTDTC. | Perm' not present in MD candidate atoms. AEENDY variable row absent.

PDF TABLE_ROW 'AEDUR | Duration of Adverse Event | Char | ISO 8601 duration | Timing | Collected duration and unit of an adverse event. Used only if collected on the CRF and not derived from start and end date/times. Example: "P1DT2H" (for 1 day, 2 hours). | Perm' not present in MD candidate atoms. AEDUR variable row absent.
PDF CODE_LITERAL 'P1DT2H' present in AEDUR table row as duration example. No MD atom in candidate set references 'P1DT2H'. PDF CODE_LITERAL 'P1DT2H' missing from MD.

PDF TABLE_ROW for AEENRF includes full codelist reference '(STENRF)', reference period definition (RFSTDTC/RFENDTC), cross-ref §4.4.7, and core=Perm. MD md_AE_assumptions_a038 (assumption 5a) covers AEENRF usage for 'Ongoing' relative to RFENDTC and references §4.4.7 but lacks the verbatim CDISC Notes text, STENRF codelist specification, and RFSTDTC/RFENDTC explicit definition from the table row.

PDF CODE_LITERAL 'STENRF' present as codelist name in AEENRF and AEENRTPT table rows. No MD atom in candidate set (assumptions.md first 30 lines) references 'STENRF'. PDF CODE_LITERAL 'STENRF' missing from MD.
PDF CODE_LITERAL 'RFSTDTC' in AEENRF CDISC Notes context (reference period start). No MD atom in candidate set references 'RFSTDTC'. PDF CODE_LITERAL 'RFSTDTC' missing from MD.

PDF CROSS_REF 'See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.' verbatim matches intent of MD cross_refs field ['Section 4.4.7'] in md_AE_assumptions_a038. MD atom includes the cross-ref inline in list item text. Slight difference: PDF says 'Section 4.4.7' vs MD cross_refs field value, but semantic content identical.

PDF TABLE_ROW for AEENRTPT specifies codelist '(STENRF)', defines it as identifying end of event before/after reference time point defined by AEENTPT, core=Perm, cross-ref §4.4.7. MD md_AE_assumptions_a038 (assumption 5a) groups AEENRTPT with AEENTPT usage together but does not separately define AEENRTPT's CDISC Notes, STENRF codelist, or Perm core as a distinct row-level entry.

PDF CROSS_REF 'See Section 4.4.7, Use of Relative Timing Variables.' for AEENRTPT row is identical in text to a014. MD md_AE_assumptions_a038 cross_refs ['Section 4.4.7'] covers both AEENRF and AEENRTPT use cases in assumption 5a. Semantically equivalent.

PDF TABLE_ROW for AEENTPT defines it as 'End Reference Time Point', char type, with description 'Description of date/time in ISO 8601 character format of the reference point referred to by AEENRTPT. Examples: "2003-12-25", "VISIT 2".' MD md_AE_assumptions_a038 mentions AEENTPT in context of 'Ongoing' relative to another date (e.g., final safety follow-up visit date) but does not capture the ISO 8601 format requirement, the reference to AEENRTPT linkage, or the examples '2003-12-25'/'VISIT 2' from CDISC Notes.

PDF NOTE '1In this column, an asterisk (*) indicates that the variable may be subject to controlled terminology. CDISC/NCI codelist values are enclosed in parentheses.' is a table footnote explaining controlled terminology notation. No MD atom in candidate set contains this footnote text. PDF CDISC Notes footnote missing from MD.

PDF HEADING 'AE – Assumptions' (heading_level 3) maps to MD md_AE_assumptions_a001 '# AE — Assumptions' (heading_level 1). Semantic content identical; minor typographic difference: PDF uses em-dash '–' vs MD uses em-dash '—'; both name the same section. Level difference (h3 vs h1) reflects document context vs standalone file structure.

PDF LIST_ITEM assumption 1 is a single paragraph. MD splits it into 4 sentences across md_AE_assumptions_a002 through a005. All content is present: ICH E2A definition (a002), scope extension/limitation (a003), protocol consistency (a004), free text or prespecified list (a005). Splitting is structural, no semantic loss. Minor: MD a002 omits 'In consultation' sentence linkage — but a003 covers it.

PDF LIST_ITEM '2. AE description and coding' is a section header/intro item. MD md_AE_assumptions_a006 renders as '2. **AE description and coding**' as a heading_level 2 element. Markdown bold formatting wraps the text but content is identical. Structural conversion from list item to heading is acceptable.

PDF: 'a. AETERM captures the verbatim term collected for the event. It is the topic variable for the AE dataset. AETERM is a required variable and must have a value.' MD md_AE_assumptions_a007 verbatim matches with leading whitespace '   a. AETERM captures...' — content identical.

PDF LIST_ITEM 2b: 'AEMODIFY is a permissible variable and should be included if the sponsor's procedure permits modification of a verbatim term for coding. The modified term is listed in AEMODIFY. The variable should be populated as per the sponsor's procedures.' MD md_AE_assumptions_a009 omits the sentence 'The modified term is listed in AEMODIFY.' — the middle clause about where the modified term is recorded is absent from the MD candidate.

PDF: 'c. AEDECOD is the preferred term derived by the sponsor from the coding dictionary...' MD md_AE_assumptions_a011 verbatim matches with leading whitespace. Content identical including Define-XML reference.
